translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Paul, apostle of Jesus Christ, according to [the] command ofGod our Saviour, and of Christ Jesus our hope,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to Timotheus, [my] true child in faith: grace, mercy, peace, fromGod our Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Even as I begged thee to remain in Ephesus, [when I was] going to Macedonia, that thou mightest enjoin some not to teach other doctrines,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: nor to turn their minds to fables and interminable genealogies, which bring questionings rather than [further]God's dispensation, which [is] in faith.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the end of what is enjoined is love out of a pure heart and a good conscience and unfeigned faith;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: which [things] some having missed, have turned aside to vain discourse,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: desiring to be law-teachers, not understanding either what they say or concerning what they [so] strenuously affirm.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now we know that the law [is] good if any one uses it lawfully,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: knowing this, that law has not its application to a righteous person, but to [the] lawless and insubordinate, to [the] impious and sinful, to [the] unholy and profane, to smiters of fathers and smiters of mothers; to murderers,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: fornicators, sodomites, kidnappers, liars, perjurers; and if any other thing is opposed to sound teaching,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: according to the glad tidings of the glory of the blessedGod, with which I have been entrusted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [And] I thank Christ Jesus our Lord, who has given me power, that he has counted me faithful, appointing to ministry him

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who before was a blasphemer and persecutor, and an insolent overbearing [man]: but mercy was shewn me because I did it ignorantly, in unbelief.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the grace of our Lord surpassingly over-abounded with faith and love, which [is] in Christ Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Faithful [is] the word, and worthy of all acceptation, that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners, of whom I am [the] first.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But for this reason mercy was shewn me, that in me, [the] first, Jesus Christ might display the whole long-suffering, for a delineation of those about to believe on him to life eternal.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now to the King of the ages, [the] incorruptible, invisible, onlyGod, honour and glory to the ages of ages. Amen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This charge, [my] child Timotheus, I commit to thee, according to the prophecies as to thee preceding, in order that thou mightest war by them the good warfare,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: maintaining faith and a good conscience; which [last] some, having put away, have made shipwreck as to faith;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of whom is Hymenaeus and Alexander, whom I have delivered to Satan, that they may be taught by discipline not to blaspheme.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I exhort therefore, first of all, that supplications, prayers, intercessions, thanksgivings be made for all men;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for kings and all that are in dignity, that we may lead a quiet and tranquil life in all piety and gravity;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for this is good and acceptable before our SaviourGod,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who desires that all men should be saved and come to [the] knowledge of [the] truth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ForGod is one, and [the] mediator ofGod and men one, [the] man Christ Jesus,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who gave himself a ransom for all, the testimony [to be rendered] in its own times;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to which I have been appointed a herald and apostle, (I speak [the] truth, I do not lie,) a teacher of [the] nations in faith and truth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will therefore that the men pray in every place, lifting up pious hands, without wrath or reasoning.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In like manner also that the women in decent deportment and dress adorn themselves with modesty and discretion, not with plaited [hair] and gold, or pearls, or costly clothing,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but, what becomes women making profession of the fear ofGod, by good works.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let a woman learn in quietness in all subjection;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but I do not suffer a woman to teach nor to exercise authority over man, but to be in quietness;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for Adam was formed first, then Eve:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Adam was not deceived; but the woman, having been deceived, was in transgression.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But she shall be preserved in childbearing, if they continue in faith and love and holiness with discretion.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The word [is] faithful: if any one aspires to exercise oversight, he desires a good work.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The overseer then must be irreproachable, husband of one wife, sober, discreet, decorous, hospitable, apt to teach;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: not given to excesses from wine, not a striker, but mild, not addicted to contention, not fond of money,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: conducting his own house well, having [his] children in subjection with all gravity;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (but if one does not know how to conduct his own house, how shall he take care of the assembly ofGod?)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: not a novice, that he may not, being inflated, fall into [the] fault of the devil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But it is necessary that he should have also a good testimony from those without, that he may fall not into reproach and [the] snare of the devil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ministers, in like manner, grave, not double-tongued, not given to much wine, not seeking gain by base means,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: holding the mystery of the faith in a pure conscience.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And let these be first proved, then let them minister, being without charge [against them].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [The] women in like manner grave, not slanderers, sober, faithful in all things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let [the] ministers be husbands of one wife, conducting [their] children and their own houses well:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for those who shall have ministered well obtain for themselves a good degree, and much boldness in faith which [is] in Christ Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These things I write to thee, hoping to come to thee more quickly;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but if I delay, in order that thou mayest know how one ought to conduct oneself inGod's house, which is [the] assembly of [the] livingGod, [the] pillar and base of the truth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And confessedly the mystery of piety is great.God has been manifested in flesh, has been justified in [the] Spirit, has appeared to angels, has been preached among [the] nations, has been believed on in [the] world, has been received up in glory.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the Spirit speaks expressly, that in latter times some shall apostatise from the faith, giving their mind to deceiving spirits and teachings of demons

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: speaking lies in hypocrisy, cauterised as to their own conscience,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: forbidding to marry, [bidding] to abstain from meats, whichGod has created for receiving with thanksgiving for them who are faithful and know the truth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For every creature ofGod [is] good, and nothing [is] to be rejected, being received with thanksgiving;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for it is sanctified byGod's word and freely addressing [him].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Laying these things before the brethren, thou wilt be a good minister of Christ Jesus, nourished with the words of the faith and of the good teaching which thou hast fully followed up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But profane and old wives' fables avoid, but exercise thyself unto piety;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for bodily exercise is profitable for a little, but piety is profitable for everything, having promise of life, of the present one, and of that to come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The word [is] faithful and worthy of all acceptation;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for, for this we labour and suffer reproach, because we hope in a livingGod, who is preserver of all men, specially of those that believe.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Enjoin and teach these things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let no one despise thy youth, but be a model of the believers, in word, in conduct, in love, in faith, in purity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Till I come, give thyself to reading, to exhortation, to teaching.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be not negligent of the gift [that is] in thee, which has been given to thee through prophecy, with imposition of the hands of the elderhood.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Occupy thyself with these things; be wholly in them, that thy progress may be manifest to all.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give heed to thyself and to the teaching; continue in them; for, doing this, thou shalt save both thyself and those that hear thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Rebuke not an elder sharply, but exhort [him] as a father, younger [men] as brethren,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: elder women as mothers, younger women as sisters, with all purity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Honour widows who are really widows;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but if any widow have children or descendants, let them learn first to be pious as regards their own house, and to render a return on their side to [their] parents; for this is acceptable in the sight ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now she who [is] a widow indeed, and is left alone, has put [her] hope inGod, and continues in supplications and prayers night and day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But she that lives in habits of self-indulgence is dead [while] living.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these things enjoin, that they may be irreproachable.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if any one does not provide for his own, and specially for those of [his] house, he has denied the faith, and is worse than the unbeliever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let a widow be put upon the list, being of not less than sixty years, [having been] wife of one man,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: borne witness to in good works, if she have brought up children, if she have exercised hospitality, if she have washed saints' feet, if she have imparted relief to the distressed, if she have diligently followed every good work.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But younger widows decline; for when they grow wanton against Christ, they desire to marry,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: being guilty, because they have cast off their first faith.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And, at the same time, they learn also [to be] idle, going about to people's houses; and not only idle, but also gossipers and meddlers, speaking things not becoming.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will therefore that the younger marry, bear children, rule the house, give no occasion to the adversary in respect of reproach.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For already some have turned aside after Satan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If any believing man or woman have widows, let them impart relief to them, and let not the assembly be charged, that it may impart relief to those [that are] widows indeed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let the elders who take the lead [among the saints] well be esteemed worthy of double honour, specially those labouring in word and teaching;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for the scripture says, Thou shalt not muzzle an ox that treadeth out corn, and, The workman [is] worthy of his hire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Against an elder receive not an accusation unless where there are two or three witnesses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Those that sin convict before all, that the rest also may have fear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I testify beforeGod and Christ Jesus and the elect angels, that thou keep these things without prejudice, doing nothing by favour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lay hands quickly on no man, nor partake in others' sins. Keep thyself pure.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Drink no longer only water, but use a little wine on account of thy stomach and thy frequent illnesses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of some men the sins are manifest beforehand, going before to judgment, and some also they follow after.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In like manner good works also are manifest beforehand, and those that are otherwise cannot be hid.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let as many bondmen as are under yoke count their own masters worthy of all honour, that the name ofGod and the teaching be not blasphemed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they that have believing masters, let them not despise [them] because they are brethren; but let them the rather serve them with subjection, because they are faithful and beloved, who profit by the good and ready service [rendered]. These things teach and exhort.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If any one teach differently, and do not accede to sound words, those of our Lord Jesus Christ, and the teaching which [is] according to piety,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: he is puffed up, knowing nothing, but sick about questions and disputes of words, out of which arise envy, strife, injurious words, evil suspicions,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: constant quarrellings of men corrupted in mind and destitute of the truth, holding gain to be [the end of] piety.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But piety with contentment is great gain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For we have brought nothing into the world: [it is] [manifest] that neither can we carry anything out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But having sustenance and covering, we will be content with these.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But those who desire to be rich fall into temptation and a snare, and many unwise and hurtful lusts, which plunge men into destruction and ruin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the love of money is [the] root of every evil; which some having aspired after, have wandered from the faith, and pierced themselves with many sorrows.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But thou, O man ofGod, flee these things, and pursue righteousness, piety, faith, love, endurance, meekness of spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Strive earnestly [in] the good conflict of faith. Lay hold of eternal life, to which thou hast been called, and hast confessed the good confession before many witnesses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I enjoin thee beforeGod who preserves all things in life, and Christ Jesus who witnessed before Pontius Pilate the good confession,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that thou keep the commandment spotless, irreproachable, until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: which in its own time the blessed and only Ruler shall shew, the King of those that reign, and Lord of those that exercise lordship;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who only has immortality, dwelling in unapproachable light; whom no man has seen, nor is able to see; to whom [be] honour and eternal might. Amen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Enjoin on those rich in the present age not to be high-minded, nor to trust on the uncertainty of riches; but in theGod who affords us all things richly for [our] enjoyment;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to do good, to be rich in good works, to be liberal in distributing, disposed to communicate [of their substance],

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: laying by for themselves a good foundation for the future, that they may lay hold of [what is] really life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: O Timotheus, keep the entrusted deposit, avoiding profane, vain babblings, and oppositions of false-named knowledge,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of which some having made profession, have missed the faith. Grace [be] with thee.
